summary: The diff implements a frontend-side ingredient database (ingredientDatabase.ts) with 70+ ingredients including harmful, caution, and beneficial classifications, updates IngredientAnalysis and ProductSuitabilityCheckerPage components with color-coded badges and compatibility scoring, and updates IngredientCard and IngredientDetailModal for correct safety display. However, the backend ingredient store (ingredient-store.mo) changes are not visible in the diff, raising questions about whether REQ-1 and REQ-2 backend requirements are fully met.

missing_requirements.requirement: Expand the backend ingredient database (ingredient-store.mo / main.mo) with at least 60 common skincare ingredients with accurate safety classifications, each queryable via existing backend API.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows a new frontend utility file (ingredientDatabase.ts) with 70+ ingredients, but no changes to ingredient-store.mo are visible. The backend/main.mo diff is truncated and does not show ingredient data being added to the Motoko backend store. It is unclear whether the backend database was actually expanded.

missing_requirements.requirement: Fix the ingredient safety classification logic in the backend so that known harmful/irritating ingredients are correctly classified as harmful/irritant rather than suitable.
missing_requirements.reason: No changes to ingredient-store.mo are shown in the diff. The backend safety classification logic fix is not evidenced in the provided diff — only frontend-side classification via ingredientDatabase.ts is visible.

hallucinated_features.description: Frontend-local ingredient database (ingredientDatabase.ts) used as the primary source for ingredient classification, with client-side compatibility score computation via computeCompatibilityScore and getVerdictFromScore utilities.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request specifies that ingredient data and scoring logic should be in the backend (ingredient-store.mo / main.mo), and that backend API signatures should be preserved. Moving the classification logic to the frontend as a local database was not requested and may bypass the backend entirely.
